mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals a breaking labor market that triggers recession fears, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging retail sales and housing starts indicate fragmented consumer demand across sectors, revealing underlying economic weakness that forces equity repricing and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over one quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, with Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production growth combined with rising inflation indicates supply bottlenecks during a late expansion, forcing the central bank to maintain restrictive policy and suppressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both series rise simultaneously, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 2.5 percent impose a high cost of capital on leveraged firms, compressing profit margins and triggering equity sell-offs that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over one quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when both conditions are met, with a negative or null Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, creating a persistent upward pressure on equity prices and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns during GDP expansion are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 2017 baseline index of 100 signals a contraction in manufacturing output that reduces corporate cash flows and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the contraction signal, or the threshold effect shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.
